title: Respect the flash and RAM budget
description: Track code, data, and stack against the device's tiny memory and keep margin, because there is no swap and overflow corrupts silently.
body: Use when adding features or libraries to a constrained MCU. Do not pull in heavyweight libraries, large buffers, or deep recursion without checking the memory map, and do not run to the edge of RAM. Actions: read the linker map after each build, watch the stack high-water mark per task, keep margin for worst-case nesting, and prefer const data in flash over RAM copies. Validation signals include a linker map that fits with headroom and stack high-water marks that never approach the guard. Risk boundary: stack overflow on an MCU silently corrupts adjacent memory and crashes unpredictably; treat shrinking stack margin as blocking and escalate before consuming the last reserve.
